     Edit the Claude source, not this file — regenerate instead. -->

> **Plane MCP pre-flight (Copilot — check this before the Plane path steps below):**
>
> Scan your available tool list for at least one tool matching `mcp__plane__*`, `plane_*`, or `mcp_plane_*`.
> - **None found → STOP.** Print: "Error: Plane MCP tools are not accessible. In VS Code, open the MCP panel (View → MCP Servers), ensure the `plane` server shows as Connected, and retry. Do not fall back to creating markdown tickets." Do not continue to the Markdown path.
> - **Found → note the active prefix** (e.g. `mcp__plane__` or `plane_`) and use it for every tool call in this session.
>
> **If `.claude/plane-config.md` exists, the Markdown path is off-limits** regardless of MCP status — never silently degrade to local TKT-*.md files when the project uses Plane.


# Review a Ticket

You are presenting a completed ticket implementation for human review. Generate a verification checklist that the user can follow to confirm the work is done correctly.

## Pre-flight: detect backend

- If `.claude/plane-config.md` exists → **Plane path**.
- Else if `.claude/ticket-config.md` + `tickets/` → **Markdown path**.
- Else: error, tell the user to run `/ticket-install`.

## Input (both backends)

The argument is a ticket ID. Bare numbers resolve via project identifier (Plane) or ticket prefix + zero-padding (markdown) as described in `/ticket-new` and `/ticket-investigate`.


## Plane path

### Pre-flight

1. Load `.claude/plane-config.md` (project id, project identifier, state + label ID maps) and `.claude/ticket-config.md` (Test / Build / Deploy / Lint commands, Main branch).
2. Retrieve the work item via `mcp__plane__retrieve_work_item_by_identifier`. 404 → error.
3. **State gate:**
   - Terminal (`Done` / `Cancelled`) → STOP with `/ticket-reopen` hint.
   - Must be `In Review`. Any other state → error with a tailored message:
     - `Backlog` → "not investigated yet, run `/ticket-investigate`".
     - `Ready` → "not implemented yet, run `/ticket-approve`".
     - `In Progress` → "still being implemented — wait for `/ticket-approve` to finish".
4. Verify you're on the feature branch. The branch name follows `ticket/<lowercased-id>-<slug>`. If the current branch doesn't match `ticket/<lowercased-id>-*`, try `git branch --list 'ticket/<lowercased-id>-*'`; if exactly one match, check it out. Otherwise ask the user.

### Phase 1: Automated checks

On the feature branch, run in order:

Checks split into **blocking** (pass/fail; any failure transitions the work item back to `In Progress`) and **informational** (reported, never blocking — `/ticket-ship` owns rebase per Plan 2).

Blocking:

1. **Tests** — Test command from `ticket-config.md`. Capture pass/fail + counts (passed, failed, skipped).
2. **Typecheck** — if configured (often part of Test or Lint).
3. **Build** — Build command. Capture pass/fail + first error if any.
4. **Lint** — if configured. Capture warnings count.
5. **No merge conflicts** — verify clean merge with main (`git merge-tree` or a throwaway `git merge --no-commit`; reset immediately). Pass if clean.

Omit any blocking check whose command is not configured (don't print "skipped" — just don't include the line).

Informational:

6. **Rebased on main** — `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/{main} HEAD`. Main = `Main branch` from ticket-config, else `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD`, else `main`. If the branch is behind, report how many commits (`git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/{main}`) and note that `/ticket-ship` will rebase before merging. Do NOT fail the review.
7. **Diff summary** — `git diff {main} --stat`.

### Phase 2: Human verification checklist

Based on the work item's **Acceptance Criteria**, **Implementation Plan**, and the actual changes (diff summary), build a checklist. Each item must be:

- **Specific** — "Navigate to /projects/123 and click 'Start'", not "test the feature".
- **Observable** — describe what the user should SEE, not what the code does.
- **Independent** — each item verifiable on its own.
- **Ordered** — steps flow naturally.

Structure:

```markdown
### Verification Checklist (for human)

#### Setup
- [ ] Build/run the project locally using the project's standard run command
- [ ] Ensure you're in the right state (logged in, on the right screen, etc.)

#### Core Functionality
- [ ] Step 1: do X, expect Y
- [ ] Step 2: do X, expect Y

#### Edge Cases
- [ ] Step N: try X with invalid input, expect error Y

#### Regression Checks
- [ ] Existing feature A still works: do X, see Y
- [ ] Existing feature B still works: do X, see Y

#### Verdict
- [ ] pass
- [ ] fail — reason:
```

The checklist must cover **every** acceptance criterion. Include regression checks for any area touched by the diff.

### Phase 3: Write to Plane

Read the current `description_html`, then `update_work_item` to append two new sections (do not truncate existing content):

```html
<h3>Automated Checks</h3>
<ul>
  <li>[x] Tests: 47 passed, 0 failed, 2 skipped</li>
  <li>[x] Typecheck: clean</li>
  <li>[x] Build: clean</li>
  <li>[x] Lint: clean (3 warnings)</li>
  <li>[x] No merge conflicts: clean</li>
  <li>[ ] <s>Tests: 3 failed</s> — <code>src/auth.test.ts:42 — expected 200, got 500</code></li>
</ul>
<blockquote>All blocking checks passed.</blockquote>
<p><em>Informational:</em></p>
<ul>
  <li>Branch rebased on <code>main</code>: yes (or: behind by 2 commits — <code>/ticket-ship</code> will rebase)</li>
  <li>Diff stat: 3 files changed, +42 / −5</li>
</ul>

<h3>Verification Checklist (for human)</h3>
<!-- the markdown-style checklist structured as above, rendered in HTML -->
```

Format rules:
- `[x]` for passing, `[ ] <s>strikethrough</s> — first error detail` for failing.
- Omit lines for unconfigured commands.
- End the Automated Checks section with a blockquote summary: either "All automated checks passed." or "`{N}` automated check(s) failed — review before shipping."

### Finish

- **No state transition.** The work item stays in `In Review` pending human verdict. (`/ticket-ship` is the next command; it verifies the checklist was filled and transitions to Done.)
- **If any BLOCKING check failed** (tests/typecheck/build/lint/merge-conflicts): transition state back to `In Progress` via `update_work_item` and stop with a message listing the failures. **Rebase-behind is NOT a blocking failure** — it's reported as informational and `/ticket-ship` will rebase.

Output:
```
{ID} Ready for Review

Branch: {branch}
Changes: {diff stat summary}

## Automated Checks
{pass/fail per check}

## Verification Checklist (manual)
{the human checklist, rendered as markdown for terminal readability}

When verified, run /ticket-ship {ID} to merge and deploy.
If issues found, describe them and I'll fix on the same branch.
```

### Rules (Plane path)

- Do NOT make code changes during review. Read-only (including no rebases — `/ticket-ship` owns rebase).
- If any **blocking** check fails → transition state to `In Progress` and report; do NOT proceed to human checklist generation.
- Rebase-behind is **informational**, not blocking. Report the commit count and move on.
- Every acceptance criterion must appear in the human checklist.
- Include regression checks for every area the diff touched.


## Markdown path

*(Unchanged. Kept in-tree until Plan 3 completes.)*

### Pre-flight Checks

- `.claude/ticket-config.md` must exist. If not, tell the user to run `/ticket-install` and stop.
- Read Test and Build commands from `.claude/ticket-config.md`.
- Ticket status MUST be `review`. If not, report the current status and stop.
- Verify the feature branch exists and is checked out.
- If the ticket is in a terminal subfolder (`shipped/`, `deferred/`, `wontfix/`), STOP and tell the user to run `/ticket-reopen {ID}` first.

### Phase 1: Generate Verification Checklist

Based on the ticket's Acceptance Criteria, Implementation Plan, and actual changes made, create a **human-testable** verification checklist. Each item must be:

- **Specific**: "Navigate to /projects/123 and click 'Start'" not "Test the feature"
- **Observable**: Describe what the user should SEE, not what the code does
- **Independent**: Each item can be verified on its own
- **Ordered**: Steps should flow naturally

#### Structure the checklist as

```markdown
## Verification Checklist (for human)

### Setup
- [ ] Build/run the project locally (use the project's standard run command)
- [ ] Ensure you are in the right state (logged in, on the right screen, etc.)

### Core Functionality
- [ ] Step 1: Do X, expect to see Y
- [ ] Step 2: Do X, expect to see Y

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Step N: Try X with invalid input, expect error message Y

### Regression Checks
- [ ] Existing feature A still works: do X, see Y
- [ ] Existing feature B still works: do X, see Y

### Verdict
- [ ] pass
- [ ] fail — reason:
```

### Phase 2: Automated verification

Run every check that can be verified without a human. Read Test, Build, and Lint commands from `.claude/ticket-config.md`. Determine the main branch: read `Main branch` from `.claude/ticket-config.md` if present, otherwise fall back to `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD`. If the result is `master`, warn the user and suggest running `/ticket-install` to migrate.

On the ticket's feature branch:

1. **Tests**: run the Test command. Capture pass/fail + counts (passed, failed, skipped).
2. **Typecheck**: run typecheck if configured (often part of Test or Lint). Capture pass/fail.
3. **Build**: run the Build command. Capture pass/fail + first error if any.
4. **Lint**: run the Lint command if configured. Capture pass/fail + warning count.
5. **Branch rebased on main**: `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/{main} HEAD`. Pass if yes.
6. **No merge conflicts**: verify clean merge with main. Pass if clean.
7. Show a diff summary: `git diff {main-branch} --stat`

Omit any check whose command is not configured — don't report "skipped."

### Phase 3: Update Ticket

1. Write the `### Automated Checks` section into the ticket's `## Verification Checklist (for human)` area, using this format:

```markdown
### Automated Checks
- [x] Tests: 47 passed, 0 failed, 2 skipped
- [x] Typecheck: clean
- [x] Build: clean
- [x] Lint: clean (3 warnings)
- [x] Branch rebased on main: yes
- [ ] ~~Tests: 3 failed~~ (`src/auth.test.ts:42 — expected 200, got 500`)

> All automated checks passed.
```

Format rules:
- `[x]` for passing, `[ ] ~~strikethrough~~` for failing with first error detail
- Omit lines for unconfigured commands
- End with `> All automated checks passed.` or `> {N} automated check(s) failed — review before shipping.`

2. Write the human verification checklist (from Phase 1) below the automated checks
3. Present it to the user in the conversation

### Finish (markdown)

Output:
```
{ID} Ready for Review

Branch: {branch}
Changes: {diff stat summary}

## Automated Checks
{automated check results — pass/fail per check}

## Verification Checklist (manual)
{the human checklist}

When verified, run /ticket-ship {ID} to merge and deploy
If issues found, describe them and I'll fix on the same branch.
```

### Rules (markdown path)

- Do NOT make code changes during review. This is read-only.
- If tests or build fail, set status back to `in-progress` and report the failures.
- The verification checklist must cover EVERY acceptance criterion from the ticket.
- Include regression checks for any area of the app that was touched.

## Compatibility Notes

- **MCP tool mapping:** In VS Code Copilot, Plane MCP tools are typically exposed as `plane_<tool_name>` (e.g. `plane_create_work_item`), not `mcp__plane__<tool_name>` (Claude Code convention). The Plane MCP pre-flight above detects which prefix is active and uses it for the session. For explicit IDs, prefer direct identifier lookup; if unavailable, use bounded project-scoped lookup + exact match.
- **AskUserQuestion:** Copilot has no equivalent structured-question tool. When the Claude source says "use AskUserQuestion", ask the user in chat with a numbered or bulleted choice and wait for a reply.
- **Parallel subagents → sequential loop:** if the Claude source fans out subagents in parallel (e.g. ticket-investigate multi-ticket, ticket-batch), run them as a sequential loop in one agent session instead. Output and behavior are identical; only wall-clock differs.
- **Backend dispatch:** the "Pre-flight: detect backend" block is authoritative in both hosts. `.claude/plane-config.md` presence routes to the Plane path; otherwise the Markdown path runs.
